title: Build a Beat
description: Create a drum pattern using the real-time recording feature with multiple sounds across the pads.

steps.title: Select Group A
steps.instruction: Press the A button to select Group A. This will be your drum group with kick, snare, hi-hat, and percussion.
steps.tip: Each group can have its own set of 12 sounds. Groups play simultaneously, so you can add bass in Group B later.

steps.title: Load Your Drum Sounds
steps.instruction: Make sure you have drum samples assigned to pads 1-4 (or use the factory sounds). Tap each pad to hear what sound is loaded.

steps.title: Set Your Tempo
steps.instruction: Tap the TEMPO/LOOP button repeatedly to tap in your desired tempo, or hold TEMPO and turn the Y knob to dial in an exact BPM.
steps.tip: 90-100 BPM for hip-hop, 120-130 BPM for house, 140+ for drum & bass.

steps.title: Enable the Metronome
steps.instruction: Press SHIFT + TEMPO to turn on the metronome. This will help you play in time while recording.

steps.title: Start Recording
steps.instruction: Press RECORD to arm recording (it will light up), then press PLAY to start the sequencer with the metronome.

steps.title: Record the Kick
steps.instruction: Play pad 1 (kick) on beats 1 and 3 as the pattern loops. Listen to the metronome and play on the downbeats.

substeps.instruction: Let the pattern loop to verify your timing

steps.title: Add the Snare
steps.instruction: Now add the snare (pad 2) on beats 2 and 4. This creates the classic backbeat.
steps.tip: Beats 2 and 4 are the off-beats - the snare should hit right after each kick.

steps.title: Add Hi-Hats
steps.instruction: Add hi-hats (pad 3) on every eighth note (twice per beat) to fill out the rhythm. Play steadily!
steps.tip: Don't worry if your timing isn't perfect - quantize will help tighten things up.

steps.title: Stop Recording
steps.instruction: Press RECORD to disable recording, then let the pattern play to hear your beat. Press PLAY to stop when you're satisfied.
steps.tip: You can continue adding layers by enabling RECORD again - it overdubs onto the existing pattern.

outcomes: Created a basic drum pattern with kick, snare, and hi-hats
Learned real-time recording with the metronome
Understanding of the overdub workflow for building patterns
